assignment_description:
Multiplication: Introduction
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ INTRO
We left off when people had learned to add and subtract pretty easily, but multiplication and division were a problem. This year, we will see how these methods were developed. We begin in the 1100s. "Moor" is an old word for a Muslim person from regions in southern Spain and northern Africa. We don't use it anymore.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.65-66 "So you wonder" - "Leonardo and Johann."

p.66-67 "When Leonardo of" - "we are taught."

➜ PRACTICE
Try multiplying 36 x 5 Leonardo's way.

➜ STUDY

Practice: The Lattice Method
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ VIEW
∞ Video Link: Lattice Multiplication (6:12)

➜ PRACTICE
Look again at the pictures. Use the lattice method to multiply 934 x 314. Which of the four methods shown matches your work? Make up another problem and use the lattice method to solve it. Check your work using our method.
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago "From the First Printed Arithmetic" p.70

Division: Scratch and Galley Methods
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.75-77 "Since this is" - "learned in school."

➜ STUDY
Look at the image. Can you find 1728 and 12? How many times will 12 go into 17? Work the problem our way. Can you find where Filippo recorded his answer?
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago "How Filippo Proved His Work" p.76

• IMPORTANT DATES
1st Arithmetic Bk, England (1522)
1st Arithmetic Bk, Mexico (1556)

Division: Adriaen and Michael
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.80 "When Filippo became" - "two centuries later."

➜ STUDY
"How Adriaen Divided." Can you find the 150 and the 6? How many times will 6 go into 15? [2 with 3 left over] Where did Adriaen record the 2 and the 3? Can you figure out the rest?

Division: Conclusion
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.81-82 "If you had" - "laughed the Crowd."

Practice
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ PRACTICE
How many 5's will go into 565? Solve using manipulatives first and then with long division.

➜ VIEW
This teacher is showing us three different algorithms for solving the same problem. An algorithm is a sort of shortcut for getting an answer without using real objects. Which do you think is easiest? Why?
∞ Video Link: Three Methods of Division (5:10)
